name: rust_build
description: Build a Rust project with cargo. Runs 'cargo build' in debug or release mode, reports build status, and shows built binaries. Replaces 3-4 separate commands: cd <project>, cargo build, find/ls to verify binaries.
when_to_use: Use when you need to compile a Rust project to verify it builds correctly before running tests, or to create a binary. Replaces the common pattern of 'cd <project> && cargo build' followed by checking the target/ directory. Use 'release' mode for optimized builds that take longer but produce faster binaries. Use '-p <package>' to build a specific package in a workspace.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Rust project (e.g. /app)

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Specific package to build in a workspace (e.g. 'boa_engine')

examples.expected: Build the Rust project in debug mode, show build status and binary info.
Build with --release flag for optimized binary.
Build only the 'boa_engine' package in debug mode.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 3-4 separate commands (cd to project, cargo build, finding binary in target/, running file/ls on binary) into a single script call. The automatic binary discovery in target/debug/ or target/release/ eliminates additional ls/find commands. Reduces round trips for Rust compilation verification, which appears 3-5 times per trajectory in Rust projects.
